title: T-088 — UI: TEC-045 Lab Results Log spec-driven screen
prompt:
# T-088 — UI: TEC-045 Lab Results Log spec-driven screen

PRD: docs/prd/03-TECHNICAL-PRD.md §0, §5, §17, prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md
Project root: /Users/mariuszkrawczyk/Projects/monopilot-kira
Prototype: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:451-546` (lab_results_log_screen)
Spec-driven: PRD/UX are canonical; adjacent `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx` is layout-primitive only.

## Goal
UI: TEC-045 Lab Results Log spec-driven screen

Spec-driven because only a per-item lab tab exists. Implement cross-item lab-results log as a Technical read model over Quality-owned lab_results. Filters: item, WO, test type, result status. Add/request flow must delegate through Quality bridge or show QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING; Technical must not write lab_results directly.

Additional acceptance detail preserved from compressed AC list:
- Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.
- Given user adds a lab result, UI delegates through Quality bridge or shows QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING; Technical does not write lab_results directly.
- UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Implementation contract
See `## Goal` + scope_files + acceptance_criteria below; preserve prototype parity, server-action semantics, RLS/org-scoping, and module red-lines listed in `## Risk red lines`.

## Files
- app/(app)/technical/lab-results/page.tsx [create]
- tests/ui/tec-045-lab-results-log.test.tsx [create]

## Acceptance criteria
1. Given the UI surface is implemented, when compared with the dedicated layout-primitive prototype `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:451-546` (`lab_results_log_screen` Quality-owned read-model list with verdict pills + ATP RLU pass/fail visualization + Open-in-QA cross-link), then it satisfies structural parity, visual parity, and interaction parity against the PRD §10.7 / UX-spec Quality-owned lab read-model log layout/flow (PRD/UX is canonical, prototype is layout-primitive only), captures Playwright screenshots and traces, passes axe checks, and applies the Technical red-line overlay: FG not FA, WIP/intermediate not PR-code, factory_spec/internal_product_spec, shared BOM SSOT, and D365 optional only. Lab results are Quality-owned read model; Technical never writes lab_results, never raises NCR, never signs off — all that lives in 09-QUALITY.
2. Given TEC-045 renders, then it satisfies the named PRD/UX spec as source of truth and does not claim exact prototype parity.
3. Given adjacent prototype primitives are reused, then screen semantics/routes/fields/states remain those required by PRD/UX.
4. Given the task is closed out, when reviewers inspect the task evidence, then these additional requirements are proven together: Given closeout is prepared, screenshots/artifacts and Playwright traces/artifacts where applicable are listed per shared UI policy.; Given user adds a lab result, UI delegates through Quality bridge or shows QUALITY_BRIDGE_MISSING; Technical does not write lab_results directly.; UI closeout includes screenshot/artifact evidence and Playwright trace/artifacts where applicable per `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md`; if Playwright is unavailable, document the blocker and provide RTL/snapshot fallback evidence.

## Test strategy
- RED RTL/Playwright tests for PRD/UX spec-driven structure and interactions.
- Run the relevant UI test for TEC-045; capture screenshot and trace artifacts.
- For UI parity, capture screenshots/artifacts and Playwright trace/video/artifacts where applicable; attach artifact paths in closeout.

## Risk red lines
- Do not block on missing prototype; use spec-driven contract explicitly.
- Do not use adjacent prototype as 1:1 source.
- FG is canonical; FA is legacy alias only — do not introduce FA-* identifiers in new schemas, code, labels, tests.
- D365 is optional integration; never use D365 IDs as hard FKs — d365_item_id is TEXT soft reference only.
- Released BOM/factory_spec edits clone-on-write a new version; never mutate an approved/released row in place.
- Shared BOM SSOT is canonical — Technical, NPD, Planning, Production, Warehouse and Finance read the same bom_headers/lines/co_products tables.
- Do not paste prototype JSX verbatim — translate to production shadcn primitives per `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md`.
- Do not use raw HTML <select> — use shadcn Select.
- Do not use inline styles — use Tailwind classes; conditional via cn()/cva().
- Do not import @radix-ui/* outside packages/ui (ESLint guard).

## Prototype parity
- Canonical anchor: `prototypes/design/Monopilot Design System/technical/spec-driven-screens.jsx:451-546` (lab_results_log_screen) — see `_meta/prototype-labels/prototype-index-technical.json` entry and `_meta/prototype-labels/translation-notes-technical.md` for shadcn translation guidance.
- Spec-driven Wave0 task: PRD §X.Y + `prototypes/design/03-TECHNICAL-UX.md` are canonical. The spec-driven JSX prototype is layout-primitive only — do not claim 1:1 visual parity.
- Evidence policy: `_meta/atomic-tasks/UI-PROTOTYPE-PARITY-POLICY.md` — closeout must include screenshot artifacts + Playwright trace/video where applicable, plus axe results or documented blocker.
